etymology.description: semantic 人(人) (rén, 'person') + phonetic 十(十) (shí) -> what
etymology.interpretation: 什(什) (shén, 'what') combines 人(人) (rén, 'person') as the semantic element, with 十(十) (shí) as the phonetic element, which traditionally suggests a quantifier or indefinite concept tied to something representing people, forming 'what'.

examples.chinese: 你在看什么(你在看什麼)？
这是什么声音(這是什麼聲音)？
我们应该做些什么(我們應該做些什麼)？
